title: The Jersey Stack — Ep 1: The Transistor (DRAFT v3)
description:
🎬 DRAFT v3 — for review only, not for public sharing.

New Jersey invented the transistor. A 20-mile stretch of NJ holds the birthplace of the thing that runs every device in your pocket, and a monument to itself just outside the Severance set. Episode 1 of The Jersey Stack follows the story from Bell Labs Murray Hill in 1947 through the Traitorous Eight walkout in 1957 — and ends back at Fort Monmouth, where Netflix is rebuilding the ground that built the hardware.

Known issues in this cut:
• Scene 29 (closing drone) has ~48 seconds of frame-hold where the video runs out before the narration ends — cutaway montage planned for v4
• Some AI-generated text (S20 newspaper headline, S26 logo sequence) may have rendering artifacts
• Scene 9 and Scene 24 motion graphics are full-length — pacing may need trimming

Chapters:
0:00 Drone Reveal & Severance Hook
1:30 Title Card
1:45 Bell Labs Murray Hill
3:15 The Vacuum Tube Problem
5:15 The Inventors
7:30 The Demo (Dec 23, 1947)
10:45 Shockley in Chicago
13:00 The June 1948 Press Conference
14:30 Fort Monmouth & InfoAge
16:00 Nobel & the Consent Decree
17:45 Shockley Semi & the Walkout
19:00 The Count-Up
19:45 Back to the Drone — Netflix Fort Monmouth

---
Part of The Jersey Stack — a five-episode series on how New Jersey invented the modern computer.
